output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's How to Recover It Fast
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, what landlords can deduct, and how to demand your money back fast.
content_outline: Landlord Withholding Security Deposit? Know Your State Deadline
Can My Landlord Keep My Security Deposit? Legal vs. Illegal Deductions
What to Do When Your Landlord Doesn't Return Your Deposit
How to Write a Security Deposit Dispute Letter That Gets Results
Tenant Rights: Next Steps If Your Landlord Still Refuses to Pay
key_points: Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14–30 days and provide an itemized deduction statement.
Landlords cannot withhold deposits for normal wear and tear; only actual damage beyond ordinary use and unpaid rent are valid deductions.
A formal demand letter is the fastest, most cost-effective way to pressure a landlord into returning your deposit before filing in small claims court.
DepositBack helps tenants generate state-compliant security deposit demand letters in minutes to recover funds faster.
output.cta: Don't let your landlord keep what's yours. Generate your state-specific security deposit demand letter with DepositBack today and recover your money faster.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlords are withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit and need immediate guidance on tenant rights and recovery options.
